# RFC-0001: ExecutionMode build_model entrypoint contract

> **Version:** 0.1.0 | **Status:** normative | **Phase:** stable

---

## 1. Summary

### [RFC-0001:C-SUMMARY] Summary (Informative) <a id="rfc-0001c-summary"></a>

An ExecutionMode is a self-contained, runnable snapshot of a model variant, forked from
a parent mode. This RFC specifies the contract that every ExecutionMode workspace MUST
satisfy so that the pipeline's evaluators (correctness, benchmark, profile) can load and
run any variant — optimized or baseline — through one uniform interface, without knowing
which optimization it embeds. The contract covers the entrypoint signature, the
post-optimization runnability and equivalence obligations, cross-workspace isolation, and
weight-file integrity. It exists because optimizations are heterogeneous yet must be
evaluated identically; a fixed entrypoint contract is what decouples the evaluators from
that heterogeneity.

*Since: v0.1.0*

---

## 2. Specification

### [RFC-0001:C-ENTRYPOINT] build_model entrypoint signature (Normative) <a id="rfc-0001c-entrypoint"></a>

Every ExecutionMode workspace MUST expose, in its entrypoint file (recorded in
ExecutionMode.entrypoint; default `build_model.py`, relative to the workspace
directory), a module-level function build_model that returns a 2-tuple
(model, tokenizer). `model` MUST be a runnable model object on the target device in
eval mode; `tokenizer` MUST be its matching tokenizer. build_model MUST accept an
optional device parameter and an optional dtype parameter, defaulting both when not
supplied.

Consumers of an ExecutionMode (correctness, benchmark, profile) MUST load the model
only through this function, MUST use the recorded entrypoint filename rather than
assuming a fixed name, and MUST NOT depend on any property of the embedded optimization
beyond this signature.

*Since: v0.1.0*

### [RFC-0001:C-RUNNABLE] Post-change runnability and equivalence (Normative) <a id="rfc-0001c-runnable"></a>

After any optimization is applied to a forked workspace, build_model() MUST still return
a runnable model, and that model MUST remain functionally equivalent to the baseline
within the project's correctness tolerance (last-token next-token logits cosine
similarity at or above the configured threshold). A change that builds but fails the
correctness check is not a valid optimization and MUST NOT be recorded as an effective
ChangeRecord.

*Since: v0.1.0*

### [RFC-0001:C-ISOLATION] Cross-workspace module isolation (Normative) <a id="rfc-0001c-isolation"></a>

Loading two different ExecutionMode workspaces in the same process MUST NOT cross-
contaminate their workspace-local modules: the model built for one workspace MUST NOT be
affected by patch or helper modules belonging to another workspace loaded earlier in the
same process. A workspace MUST therefore defer the import and application of its own
workspace-local patch modules to build_model() call time rather than to module import
time.

*Since: v0.1.0*

### [RFC-0001:C-WEIGHTS] Hardlinked weights are immutable in place (Normative) <a id="rfc-0001c-weights"></a>

Large weight files in a forked workspace are HARDLINKED from the parent (same inode).
An optimization that must mutate weights MUST write NEW files and rewire build_model()
to load them; it MUST NOT edit a hardlinked weight file in place, because doing so
silently corrupts the parent mode and every sibling fork sharing that inode.

*Since: v0.1.0*

### [RFC-0001:C-SMOKE] No ChangeRecord over a non-loadable workspace (Normative) <a id="rfc-0001c-smoke"></a>

An apply-agent MUST NOT report a ChangeRecord for a workspace whose entrypoint cannot be
loaded: build_model MUST be importable from the workspace entrypoint without raising. If
loading the entrypoint raises, the agent MUST repair the workspace before reporting any
change, and MUST NOT report a change over a broken workspace.

*Since: v0.1.0*

### [RFC-0001:C-STACKING] Optimizations stack additively (Normative) <a id="rfc-0001c-stacking"></a>

An applied optimization MUST stack on top of the optimizations already recorded in the
parent mode's change_log. It MUST NOT undo or duplicate a previously applied
optimization. The change_log is append-only: each accepted optimization adds exactly one
ChangeRecord to the records inherited from the parent.

*Since: v0.1.0*
